comment.body:
Example: August of 2007

Hit a low on August 16th 2007 and reversed +6% by the next day. Stabilized and continued higher. Then, proceeded to steadily rally to new ATH by October 

I only use that example because bear traps and markets can be extreme. But you go to more recent like in 2022. It took tech 3 days in some cases to reverse 10% from the lows

comment.body: My feel is that the dealers must be getting shorter and shorter delta down here. :: Here lie possibly very wrong ideas:::  Could we argue that they need volume to be hedging effectively without moving the market up? We have seen such low volume down here they have likely not been hedging against big sellers, if we don't see big sellers down here the MM's will eventually be forced to buy? Particularly as it get's later in the day. We can safely say the odds don't lie in favour of going lower, and if we haven't seen big absorption of sellers or  bounced by the evening the odds for a squeeze might get decent. Thoughts???

comment.body:
Ya but those rallies occurred after the market dropped more than it has here. For an equivalent bounce from where the market bottomed from those lows in 2022 would mean we’d need to be trading around 5330. So this bounce we just saw could still be part of the initial wave down.

But, there are some names holding onto a high lows here, like NVDA, AAPL, GE, TSLA, etc. Now that we are close to Alma’s 5567 what’s next? Also have the probability of some stabilizing news on April 2nd. 

However, Monday seems entirely probable for a an open and trade lower. Tough position right here. 2 days can make a massive difference in this environment

comment.body:
Couple things im seeing 
- SKEW lower <-- less "crash event" hedging (i think this is a measure of 5 delta puts)
- GLD higher (this makes sense to me) 
- TNX down - If inflation was the main thing this should go higher
- MAGS ETF is sitting at the low before the bounce, NVDA is pretty strong here
- IV/RV should be about neutral now. I was under the impression that RV would come down to meet IV and create a crush. If Monday comes green then that gives you you're Vol crush as RV goes down but IV remains elevated

comment.body: I expect a market rally to begin on Monday to 5850+. The flight of foreign investors from U.S. equities—sustained selling in the spot market—was the driving force, which is why the VIX didn’t rise much. The impact was particularly severe on the MAG7. In the process, the dollar declined more than U.S. Treasury rates, but now the dollar is showing some resilience. I see it as a positive sign.
